entry.title: Unalienable
entry.summary: We're working on a time capsule. Did you know that? America…
entry.content:
We're working on a time capsule.

Did you know that?

America is.

I'm not directly involved at all.

But I am an American.

So I kinda am.

Using the constitutional republic "We".

So I'm kinda, technically, adjacently, taking credit for it.

Even though I did nothing.

Boy, does that sound familiar.

Make sense?

It's to be buried on July 4th this year.

Only to be opened 250 years thereafter.

On the five hunny.

On five Benjamins.

Want to know what's in it?

Some cool and weird stuff.

Like an iPhone.

Synthetic DNA (seriously).

All 50 states get to represent.

DC, too.

They put in rocks.

Cloth.

Music.

Copies of our founding documents.

Self-evident truths that seem to require an explanation these days.

Hopefully not in two fiddy.

Life.

Liberty.

The pursuit (not the guarantee) of happiness.

We can't take it away.

Unless we have to or want to.

Then we will.

No biggie.

Someone once said America is the first country to be founded on a good idea.

And I always liked that.

I don't know if we're the first.

But I like the last part.

Founded on a good idea.

We hold these truths.

On our sacred Honor.

🇺🇸

entry.title: Ever Laugh And Fart At The Same Time?
entry.summary: Alright. I admit it. I've seen those videos where someone will…
entry.content:
Alright.

I admit it.

I've seen those videos where someone will test the generosity of a person.

Ask 'em for a dollar or something.

Get rejected a hundred times.

Find one who gleefully gives it.

Person filming takes it.

Walks away.

Comes back dramatically.

Gives the money back.

And then gifts them an iPhone.

Or something.

The tears flow.

Trumpets blare.

But here's the weird part.

A lot of these videos don't end there.

They don't end at the elation of ecosystems.

You would think you cut it here.

But no.

The giver then says something like, "If you could tell the world one thing, what would you say?"

And I always think, what a terrible thing to ask someone.

Right on the spot.

Right then and there.

Off the top of their elated inflated heads.

So I've been giving out a lot of dollars lately.

No, you haven't.

To anyone who asks, really.

Stop lying.

Not in hopes of getting a dollar free iPhone.

We call that a fringe benefit.

But for my moment.

My moment.

"Could I have a dollar?"

Oh snap.

Of course!!!

Here it comes.

"If you could tell the world one thing, what would it be?"

[Nervous toot].

Oh no.

Can we do a retake?

Hey!

Aww crap.

📱 

Sweet.

entry.title: Unctuous
entry.summary: Well, hello. How are you? Have you eaten? Come, sit down…
entry.content:
Well, hello.

How are you?

Have you eaten?

Come, sit down and join us.

We were just about to dine on some home cooked Korean food.

Prepared by yours truly.

The best cuisine on the planet.

Would you do us the honor of having a little less food to throw out?

Yeah, pretty sure you have never said that in your life.

Oh bother.

It's good to have an imagination.

Yes?

💭

Stuffing our grocery holes is a term I heard someone from Maine use to refer to the art of consumption.

Stuffing our grocery holes.

That just tickles me.

So I use it from time to time.

To mixed reception.

(My sweet spot).

I don't have a point today.

Like many days.

I'm just here. 

Taking it all in.

I am simply amused with myself.

You got to be your number one fan.

You got to be your number one fan.

Woah.

Right?

Clear eyes.

Full belly.

Always win.

After nap.

Win after nap.

Plenty of time to win.

After nap.

💤

entry.title: Long Gamer
entry.summary: How does a person, who almost passed, still play the long…
entry.content:
How does a person, who almost passed, still play the long game?

That's a good question.

One I'm dancing with.

Every.

Single.

Day.

So far, it's been to slow down.

Smell the tulips.

Wiggle toes in carpet, grass, water, and sand.

And immerse myself in my family's frenzied, kinetic, jubilance.

And to look skyward.

Heavenward.

And smile.

You would think there is a sense of urgency here.

As every second goes.

But that's short thinking.

Instant gratification.

Everyone thinks this way.

And I kinda see why now.

Now!!!

But that's not me.

Hasn't been me in a while.

In ages.

I believe in planned spontaneity.

In asymmetric bets.

In an apex opus.

No all-ins, though.

Not any more.

I think in generations, still.

Future, though.

Always future.

Perhaps a weakness.

Certainly a strength.

I don't think I've ever needed much.

Never wanted much.

Some might disagree.

Peace is an interesting concept.

I have achieved it.

Contrary to what you read here.

This is just nonsense.

But it's mine.

Of course I speak only of the material in this context of limited want.

Spirituality is an infinite want.

An infinite need.

You gotta have faith a faith a faitha.

You gotta have faith.

Bay Beeah!!

👢e

My body healing; my soul larger than ever.

These are attributes I hope to pass on.

When I do.

Before and after, too.

🦁

entry.title: Integrity, Intelligence, and Energy
entry.summary: And another thing. Didn't you just muse? I'm on a roll…
entry.content:
And another thing.

Didn't you just muse?

I'm on a roll here.

Repeat after me:

Rejection is not the horrible thing.

Rejection is not the horrible thing.

What is the horrible thing?

What is the horrible thing?

Stop.

Choosing, or being chosen by, horrible things.

There is blessing in rejection.

In failure.

In a lot of near misses.

Reframe your rejections.

Reevaluate your failures.

Sometimes the worst thing is the "Yes."

No?

To unanswered prayers.

You might be dodging bullets.

But by the grace of God...

Because sometimes, a lot of times, it's the hits that end of being fatal.

You want to know the greatest curse that looks like a blessing?

May your every wish come true.

On that happy note.

Al out.

♾

entry.title: Not For Everyone
entry.summary: How many people "get" you? You know what I'm asking here?…
entry.content:
How many people "get" you?

You know what I'm asking here?

I think that's what you're looking for.

If you're wondering.

You need to find your people.

There are two types of people.

Maybe more.

There are those who just stare at you when you are sharing a musing.

And then there are those who are on the floor.

Rolling.

At its punchline.

I tend to like the latter more.

I mean, don't be fake, but I like the belly bwahahaers.

My favorite compliments are those who complain their stomaches hurt from laughing so much.

It just tickles me elbow.

I can tell a fake laugh from a real one a country mile away.

So don't be throwing any courtesy giggles over here.

I don't want any.

But I'm taking notes.

The people that get "it" and have "it."

That's who you want to be.

The people that "get" you.

That's who you want to find.

entry.title: Big Bang Shot
entry.summary: You still talking about moonshots? What's that now? To the moon!…
entry.content:
You still talking about moonshots?

What's that now?

To the moon!

Like you Ralph Kramden.

The better term is Marsshot.

Former owner of the Reds?

Ok, Marsshot looks terrible.

Double s's.

But I think that's what they'll call it.

No longer aiming for "just" the moon.

Going further.

Farther.

Me?

I'm gunning for Alpha Centauri.

Seems doable.

AC Starfaring.

No use, never use, in playing short.

A trillion is a million millions.

Just thought I'd share that.

🚀

entry.title: AlexX
entry.summary: SpaceX goes public tomorrow. If you're into that sort of thing.
entry.content:
SpaceX goes public tomorrow.

If you're into that sort of thing.

Going public and sheet.

As a private eye guy, I will be investigating.

When it occurs to me.

Sometime mid-afternoon.

Early evening.

You'd think SpaceX was all about space.

And as a Starfaring fellow, that I'd be all in.

But it's not.

And I'm not.

It's mostly about space.

Rockets.

Which generates money.

And satellites.

Which makes money.

To turn earth into a Dyson Sphere.

But it's also about ai.

Which is costing them a fortune.

The ai has to help build out Mars.

But all I know is ai in general will win, I just don't know who that means.

Like automobiles and car companies.

Internet and software.

Of course, fortunes are often relative.

Levels, man, levels...

The fortune lost thus far should be made up for (exponentially) tomorrow.

People are all over the place on this IPO.

Salvation or ruin.

Boom or bust.

Both?

Probably both.

Polarization at its finest.

I got my Coke Zero ready.

Cheers.

Almost game time.

Time for war paint.

If you're into that.

🎲

entry.title: Too Good To Be True
entry.summary: It occurs to me, that a lot of my stories are…
entry.content:
It occurs to me, that a lot of my stories are old.

They are old.

Like you.

I'm not old, though.

I'm oldish.

This is done intentionally.

With intention.

Most of today's stories go like this.

I was with my lovely daughter the other day.

We were hanging out.

As we are wont to do.

When she asked, "How is your day going?"

"It is going wonderful. How is yours?"

"It's great!"

"Good."

"Do you want to know why I asked how your day is going?"

"Of course."

"Because I wanted to start a conversation with you."

"Well, it worked. What should we talk about next?"

And off we went.

These are my glory days.

My favorite stories days.

They are so good, I sometimes keep them hidden.

It sometimes feels like a dream.

So for now, I'll mostly share old stories.

New musings.

Old stories.

And sometimes, only once in a while, tell you about my day.

entry.title: We Could Move to South Oregon
entry.summary: Do I tell more stories? Or do I tell more musings?…
entry.content:
Do I tell more stories?

Or do I tell more musings?

Probably both.

Probably both.

When I was working in Menlo Park, in the heart of venture financing, I was working for a law partner who advised, "You need to listen to your inner voice. What's it saying to you, right now?"

And I heard it.

My inner voice.

It said one word.

One word only.

Calmly.

Definitively.

"Run."

Now trust me, no one missed me leaving there.

And I hate vehemently dislike running.

But I thank the partner.

And your inner voice.

Mmhmm.

Off I went.

I had achieved a dream out there in Silicon Valley.

But it wasn't mine.

It was never mine.

And in a way that I am, as Koreans are, I honored my family by becoming a lawyer.

And then moved across the country to do it.

The former being the Korean part of me.

The latter being the me part.

But if I'm honest, I always wanted to live in California.

And I never knew what I wanted to be when I grew up.

So law school in Cali felt like an answer.

Or at least, a three year delay in having to find one.

This is, in hindsight, terrible reasoning.

But I think terrible reasoning is a foundational component of youth.

Of middle and old age, too.

All of that to say, as I always say, everything - and I mean everything - leads me to right here.

Right now.

Living out in SV reinforced my love of technology and possibility.

Leaving it reinforced my love of a simple life.

Besides, with these little slabs of silicon, you can do anything anywhere in the Actuverse.

Is he making up words?

To chasing dreams.

Finding them.

And leaving them by the ocean.

Hallelujah.
